citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
Several medications are expired or do not have an expiration date on the container.
-Isopropyl Alcohol expired 12/2025
-Pyrantel expired March 2025
-Fenbendazole in a jar with no expiration date
-Toltrazuril in a container with no expiration date
-Ivermectin expired March 2025
Expired medications do not ensure that the medications are effective at treating disease or illness and the health of the
animal may suffer. Each dealer shall use appropriate methods to prevent, control, diagnose, and treat diseases and
injuries. The facility must ensure all medications are not expired.
To be corrected by: 30-March-2026

Three litters of puppies do not have an individual identification number assigned to them on cage cards. The licensee
states he will sometimes wait until the puppies are weaned to assign them a number. The following litters did not have
identification: 6 puppies born February 28, 2026; 7 puppies born March 7, 2026; 8 puppies born March 20, 2026. Proper
dog identification is required and can be important in animal health tracking. Ensure that each puppy under 16 weeks of
age is properly identified in a timely manner.
To be corrected by: 30-March-2026

There are whelping boxes and doorways that have been heavily chewed in 8 enclosures housing a total of 13 adults and
34 puppies. Heavily chewed surfaces are not able to be readily cleaned and sanitized and may cause disease hazards to
the animals. The licensee must ensure that all surfaces in contact with the dogs and cats to be readily cleaned and
sanitized in accordance with § 3.11(b) of this subpart, or be replaceable when worn or soiled.
To be corrected by: 9-April-2026

The licensee identified a female dog in labor with one puppy born housed with 3 other adult dogs. This female was due
yesterday but the licensee said he did not think she was pregnant so he left her in the enclosure. Housing puppies with
adult dogs that are not their dam or foster dam may result in injuries due to compatibility problems.
Puppies under 4 months of age or less may not be housed in the same primary enclosure with adult dogs other than their
dams or foster dams. The licensee must ensure the puppies listed above are only housed with their dam, foster dam or
other puppies under 4 months of age at all times.
This was fixed at the time of inspection.

citations.desc: Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control.
citations.narrative:
The licensee does not sanitize the sheltered primary enclosures, feeders or waterers for all dogs every 2 weeks. The
licensee states sanitizing is done approximately two times per year with soap. Failure to sanitize enclosures may increase
the risk of disease hazards towards the dogs.
Used primary enclosures and food and water receptacles for dogs must be sanitized at least once every 2 weeks and
more often if necessary to prevent an accumulation of dirt, debris, food waste, excreta, and other disease hazards. Hard
surfaces must be sanitized using one of the following methods: live steam under pressure or washing with hot water (at
least 180° F) and soap or detergent. The licensee must clean and sanitize the primary enclosures and ensure that all
aspects of the enclosure are maintained in a clean and sanitized condition in order to support the health of the dogs.
To be corrected by: 2-April-2026

The facility is not always following the deworming plan for puppies.
-5 puppies born 30-December-2025- Safeguard not done day 54-56, Ivermectin day 56 not done
-7 puppies born 13-February-2026- Toltrazuril not done on day 24
-3 puppies born 5-February-2026- Toltrazuril not done day 4 and 14, pyrantel not done on day 5 and 12
Sampling of dogs for parasites is necessary to ensure the animals are receiving adequate veterinary care. Each dealer
must follow an appropriate program of veterinary care for dogs that is developed, documented in writing, and signed by
the attending veterinarian and includes sampling and treatment of parasites and other pests.
To be corrected by: 27-March-2026
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee.
n

narrative:
2.40(b)(2) 
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
Several medications are expired or do not have an expiration date on the container. 
-Isopropyl Alcohol expired 12/2025 
-Pyrantel expired March 2025 
-Fenbendazole in a jar with no expiration date 
-Toltrazuril in a container with no expiration date 
-Ivermectin expired March 2025 
Expired medications do not ensure that the medications are effective at treating disease or illness and the health of the 
animal may suffer. Each dealer shall use appropriate methods to prevent, control, diagnose, and treat diseases and 
injuries. The facility must ensure all medications are not expired. 
To be corrected by: 30-March-2026 
2.50(a)(2) 
   
Time and method of identification. 
Three litters of puppies do not have an individual identification number assigned to them on cage cards. The licensee 
states he will sometimes wait until the puppies are weaned to assign them a number. The following litters did not have 
identification: 6 puppies born February 28, 2026; 7 puppies born March 7, 2026; 8 puppies born March 20, 2026. Proper 
dog identification is required and can be important in animal health tracking. Ensure that each puppy under 16 weeks of 
age is properly identified in a timely manner. 
To be corrected by: 30-March-2026 
3.6(a)(2)(ix) 
   
Primary enclosures. 
There are whelping boxes and doorways that have been heavily chewed in 8 enclosures housing a total of 13 adults and 
34 puppies. Heavily chewed surfaces are not able to be readily cleaned and sanitized and may cause disease hazards to 
the animals. The licensee must ensure that all surfaces in contact with the dogs and cats to be readily cleaned and 
sanitized in accordance with § 3.11(b) of this subpart, or be replaceable when worn or soiled. 
To be corrected by: 9-April-2026 
3.7(c)

Compatible grouping. 
The licensee identified a female dog in labor with one puppy born housed with 3 other adult dogs. This female was due 
yesterday but the licensee said he did not think she was pregnant so he left her in the enclosure. Housing puppies with 
adult dogs that are not their dam or foster dam may result in injuries due to compatibility problems. 
Puppies under 4 months of age or less may not be housed in the same primary enclosure with adult dogs other than their 
dams or foster dams. The licensee must ensure the puppies listed above are only housed with their dam, foster dam or 
other puppies under 4 months of age at all times. 
This was fixed at the time of inspection. 
3.11(b)(2) 
   
Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control. 
The licensee does not sanitize the sheltered primary enclosures, feeders or waterers for all dogs every 2 weeks. The 
licensee states sanitizing is done approximately two times per year with soap. Failure to sanitize enclosures may increase 
the risk of disease hazards towards the dogs. 
Used primary enclosures and food and water receptacles for dogs must be sanitized at least once every 2 weeks and 
more often if necessary to prevent an accumulation of dirt, debris, food waste, excreta, and other disease hazards. Hard 
surfaces must be sanitized using one of the following methods: live steam under pressure or washing with hot water (at 
least 180° F) and soap or detergent. The licensee must clean and sanitize the primary enclosures and ensure that all 
aspects of the enclosure are maintained in a clean and sanitized condition in order to support the health of the dogs. 
To be corrected by: 2-April-2026 
3.13(a)(3) 
   
Veterinary care for dogs. 
The facility is not always following the deworming plan for puppies.  
-5 puppies born 30-December-2025- Safeguard not done day 54-56, Ivermectin day 56 not done 
-7 puppies born 13-February-2026- Toltrazuril not done on day 24 
-3 puppies born 5-February-2026- Toltrazuril not done day 4 and 14, pyrantel not done on day 5 and 12 
Sampling of dogs for parasites is necessary to ensure the animals are receiving adequate veterinary care. Each dealer 
must follow an appropriate program of veterinary care for dogs that is developed, documented in writing, and signed by 
the attending veterinarian and includes sampling and treatment of parasites and other pests.  
To be corrected by: 27-March-2026 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee.
